title: PayZapp x IPL
subtitle: Turning a payments app into a live cricket companion on the occasion of IPL 2024

blocks.body:
IPL is a moment — not a month. For PayZapp, HDFC Bank's payments super-app, it was also an opportunity: a built-in spike in attention, emotion, and screen time that could be converted into activation, engagement, and transaction behaviour.
The brief: design a full-season experience layer on top of the app — one that felt native to cricket, not bolted on. Three campaigns, a redesigned app skin, and the infrastructure to survive match-night traffic.

The app had no reason to be opened during a match.
PayZapp's core loop was transactional — you opened it to pay, then left. IPL gave us a window to change that behaviour: to make the app feel alive, contextual, and worth coming back to. Two real challenges shaped the work:

blocks.headline: Campaigns without a clear entry point stay invisible
blocks.body: Banners, nudges, and deep links had to work together to surface the right thing at the right moment — without feeling like spam.

blocks.headline: A sponsorship this visible couldn't afford visible failures
blocks.body: IPL sends traffic spikes unlike anything a payments app usually handles. The design had to degrade gracefully — hiding heavy modules, collapsing banners when APIs failed, never letting the UX break under load. The engineering and design problems were the same problem.

Three campaigns. One consistent language.
The design challenge wasn't just making things look IPL-themed. It was building a coherent system where three very different campaigns — acquisition, real-time urgency, and long-form gamification — could coexist inside the same app without feeling like three different products. And doing it at a scale the sponsorship made unavoidable.
We started by mapping each campaign to a distinct emotional state:

blocks.headline: Jeeto Dhan Dhana Dhan
blocks.body: Acquisition energy. New user, first reward, instant payoff. Design posture: remove every point of friction between arriving and getting something.

blocks.headline: Strategic Timeout
blocks.body: Live urgency. 1000 spots. Match in progress. Open now. Design posture: communicate scarcity without anxiety. The window is real; the clock is visible. And critically — this was our naming rights moment. The Strategic Timeout was PayZapp's on-air. The in-app experience had to match that energy.

blocks.headline: Score & Win
blocks.body: Progress and pride. Accumulate, track, compete over a season. Design posture: a persistent presence inside the app — something always telling you where you stand.

The direction we aligned on: a full event layer across the app, not just campaign landing pages. IPL theming on the icon, the splash, the QR screen, the payment success moment. Every surface saying cricket season — so the sponsorship wasn't just something users saw on TV, but something they felt inside the product. A campaign landing page users have to find is already a failed campaign.

Before campaigns, we established the visual language. IPL blue and gold tones, kinetic cricket motifs on the splash, a cricket-themed app icon, and a texture layer that showed up across banners, QR screens, and payment moments.

The goal wasn't decoration — it was continuity. A user watching PayZapp's name during a Strategic Timeout on JioCinema, then opening the app, needed to feel like they'd stepped into the same world, not switched contexts entirely. Sponsorship creates brand recognition. Design had to close the gap between recognition and experience.
